narrative.headline: 2019 Ram 1500: electrical and steering top the complaint list
narrative.summary: Electrical system faults make up the largest share of owner complaints, with steering failures close behind — both categories include reports of fires and crashes. Owners most commonly describe sudden loss of vehicle control or unexpected behavior immediately after taking delivery, sometimes within days of purchase. Engine and powertrain complaints frequently overlap, with reports of unintended acceleration accompanied by multiple warning lights illuminating at once.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the truck becoming inoperable shortly after delivery, with the vehicle failing to respond to driver inputs such as reversing out of a driveway. · 4 fire · 7 crash · 5 injured

issues.sample: THE STEERING WENT OUT DUE TO A PCM FAILURE.  THE DEALER SAID THAT THE PCM SHUT DOWN THE STEERING DUE TO SENSING TOO MUCH VOLTAGE FROM GENERATOR TO PROTECT THIS UNIT.  I WAS TRAVELING ON US HIGHWAY 400 AT 50MPH WHEN IT WOULD NOT STEER LEFT OR RIGHT.  IT WOULD ONLY GO STRAIGHT.  I
issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Owners report sudden complete loss of steering while driving at highway speeds, with dealers attributing the failure to the PCM shutting down steering in response to a voltage condition. · 11 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle accelerating on its own immediately after taking delivery, accompanied by multiple safety warning lights activating at the same time. · 4 fire · 4 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report unintended acceleration at delivery along with multiple safety lights coming on, with repair attempts including throttle-related component replacement. · 1 fire · 2 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Fewer reports (127, 9%) but the most crashes of any area - 15, plus 2 fires and 7 injuries. Test braking thoroughly before you commit.
